sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 13g of fat. omega 3 is exactly the kind of fat support you want from a meat. for meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side: easy to build a strong meal around because it works as a genuine centrepiece instead of needing lots of nutritional excuses. very satisfying as a protein source because that makes it practical in real meals. has unusually strong trust with viewers because people already read it as a serious healthy-food choice, which matches its overall case.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides: can be expensive because price can make it less practical. quality varies with sourcing because sourcing changes the real-world quality. needs good storage and handling because it is less convenient than shelf-stable options.

closing.summary: Salmon has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when you want efficient protein and can accept the category tradeoffs that come with it.
